item.name: Screamspore
entries: This round grey mass of mycelia shivers softly in response to sound. Once per day, you can shake the fungal mass as an action to dislodge spores in a space adjacent to you, which will grow an unaligned shrieker over the course of one minute.

item.name: Heartseed
entries: This large heart-shaped wooden seed beats softly, its core glowing with a soft green light; To attune to this item, you must hold it over your heart for the entire attunement period, at which point it painlessly burrows beneath your skin. While attuned to this item, you gain the following properties, with spells using Constitution as your spellcasting ability:

entries.name: New Bloom
entries: While attuned to the Heartseed, your type becomes Plant.

entries.name: Razorvines
entries: The Heartseed has 3 charges; You can spend 1 or more charges to innately cast the spell {@spell ensnaring strike} as a bonus action. The spell's level equals the number of charges you expend. You regain 2 charges every time you take a short rest in direct sunlight.

entries.name: Proliferation
entries: Once per long rest, you can cast {@spell polymorph} as an action targeting yourself, but can only transform into a shambling mound.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The Heartseed can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the Heartseed ends, causing it to painlessly emerge from your chest. If you die while attuned to the Heartseed, your corpse becomes a shambling mound allied with Avassh.

item.name: Gibbering Flail
entries: This head of this terrifying weapon is a solid ball of gibbering mouther - a mass of flesh studded with eyes and teeth that mutters and wails softly to itself.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, when you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 hit points with this weapon, it gains 1 charge. The {@item Flail|PHB} can store up to 2 charges.

Upon hitting a creature with the {@item Flail|PHB}, you can expend a charge to release a wave of gibbering madness. Every creature in a 10ft cone in the direction of the hit creature must make a DC13 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they come under the effects of the {@spell confusion} spell. You can expend 2 charges at once to increase the range of the cone to 20ft.

item.name: Limic
entries: This small symbiotic mimic must be consumed to attune to it. Upon attunement, it moves around your bones, mimicking the flesh and blood to painlessly extend your limbs as though they were made of liquid. While attuned to the Limic, you gain the following properties:

entries.name: Elastic Form
entries: When you make a melee weapon attack, you can command the Limic to extend your arms like whips, adding 10ft to the range of your melee weapon attacks until the end of your turn. You can use this a number of times per day equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Adhesive Grip
entries: You have advantage on all ability checks made to non-magically grapple or maintain a grapple.

entries.name: Mimic Mimic
entries: You gain the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with mimics. Their initial attitude towards you does not change.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The Limic can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the Limic ends, as it seeps out of you. If you die while the Limic is inside you, it bursts out upon consuming your limb bones, becoming a mimic allied with the daelkyr.

item.name: Slidestone
entries: This stone appears so perfectly, flawlessly smooth that it's distracting and oddly confusing. While it is on your person, the ground you stand on feels so smooth to you that you can simply will yourself to move along it, gaining a normal movement speed whilst appearing to ice-skate on normal terrain. You are still affected by rough terrain, which causes the effect to end. Jarringly.

item.name: Spaceshift Gauntlets
entries: These stone gauntlets bind to the flesh of your hands, allowing you to warp and shift the geometry of space. When you hit an enemy with an unarmed strike, or successfully shove them, you can choose to move them 5ft in any horizontal direction. If this movement is stopped by a solid object, they instead take an extra {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The Gauntlets can't be removed from you while you're attuned to them, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to them. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the Gauntlets end.

item.name: Stonemeld
entries: This small ball of granite appears to show flesh beneath the small cracks in its surface. To attune to this item, you must hold it against your skin for the entire attunement period, at which point the ball cracks, covering your skin in an ultra-thin layer of granite. While attuned to this item, you gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Earthmeld
entries: You can innately cast the spell Meld into
Stone once per long rest, which you can choose to cast as a reaction upon rolling initiative.

entries.name: Semi-Petrified
entries: You gain advantage on saving throws made to avoid petrification and the petrification process.

entries.name: Granite Skin
entries: You gain natural armor equal to 12 + your Constitution modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The Stonemeld can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the Stonemeld ends, causing it to flow back together into a ball of granite. If you die while attuned to the Stonemeld, your body is {@condition petrified}.

item.name: Swarmstriders
entries: These carapace boots have rows of spurs on the back like the legs of a grasshopper. As a bonus action, you can target two allies within 30ft of you that are not {@condition deafened} by rasping the spurs to create a Swarmsong melody. As a reaction, each creature can choose to copy the exact movement you make until the end of your turn until they would exceed their own movement speed. This copied movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. A creature cannot copy movement such as flight unless it too can fly. You can use this feature once per long rest.

item.name: Chitterwing
entries: This large iridescent dragonfly appears to have immensely oversized diaphanous wings. To attune to this item, you must let the Chitterwing lie on your back for the entire attunement period, after which it will burrow beneath your flesh and meld with your spine. While attuned to this item, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain a fly speed of 30ft and can choose to hover.
You gain resistance to thunder damage.
You do not provoke opportunity attacks if you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action while flying.

entries.name: Symbiotic Nature
entries: The Chitterwing can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the Chitterwing ends, causing it to painlessly emerge from your back. If you die while attuned to the Chitterwing, your corpse keeps the 30ft fly speed and attempts to flee.

item.name: Irradiant Torc
entries: This band of golden metal stings slightly to the touch. Once per turn, upon restoring any number of hit points to a creature, you can choose to deal radiant damage equal to your proficiency bonus to a creature of your choice within 10ft of the target.
Additionally, you can cast the {@spell Light} cantrip.

item.name: Daybreaker
entries: This Giant '{@item light hammer|PHB}' burns with latent radiance absorbed from the Sunseal. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which sheds {@quickref Vision and Light||2||bright light} in 20ft radius and {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} for a further 20ft when wielded. Additionally, you can cast the spell {@spell spirit shroud|TCE} (radiant) as a bonus action once per day using this weapon, but take {@damage 1d8} radiant damage when you do.

item.name: Soulbound Studded Leather
entries: The surface of this jet-black armor appears to shift and flow softly as though countless shadows are trapped within. When you cast a spell from your Patron's expanded spell list while wearing this armor, you can choose to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

item.name: Umbral Shroud
entries: This diaphanous cloak drinks in the light and feels constantly slick to the touch. Each is soaked in the liquid negative energy of the Sacrament and worn by a powerful member of the Umbragen. While you are attuned to this cloak, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain resistance to necrotic damage.
While in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} or darkness, you can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a bonus action.

While you are in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} or darkness, you can magically teleport as a bonus action to an unoccupied space within 60ft of you that you can see and is also in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} or darkness. You can use this feature once per long rest.

item.name: The Reyefyl
entries: This horrific {@item staff|PHB} is formed from an obsidian spear impaled through the twitching eye of a spectator. While holding this {@item staff|PHB}, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.

The {@item staff|PHB} has 6 charges for the following properties. It regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a roll of 8 or lower, after casting the spell, the eye then casts it again at you using your spell save DC or spell attack modifier.

entries.name: Spells
entries: You can use an action to expend 1 or more of the {@item staff|PHB}'s charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@spell cause fear|XGE} (1 charge), {@spell hold person} (2 charges), {@spell inflict wounds} with a range of 30ft (1 charge), or {@spell confusion} (4 charges).

item.name: Forlorn Harpoon
entries: This unnerving bone-tipped harpoon is an arm cut off from The Lonely, complete with sodden grey flesh. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, when you use this weapon to make a ranged weapon attack and hit, you can choose to pull the the target up to 20 feet towards you. If the target is adjacent to you as a result, it becomes {@condition grappled} if it is a Large or smaller creature (escape DC 15). Until the grapple is broken, you can continue to make melee weapon attacks against the target with this weapon, but cannot use it to attack a different target without breaking the grapple.

item.name: Astral Lodestar
entries: This carved cube of {@condition petrified} astral dreadnought is intrinsically linked to Demiplane Doorways; by holding this item in front of a Demiplane Doorway and {@status concentration||concentrating} on it for one minute as though on a spell, you can temporarily change the portal to a Doorway to for 1 minute. Additionally, once per day, you can use an action to focus on the Lodestar and detect the location and direction of all Demiplane Doorways in a 1-mile radius.

item.name: Scalehide Gauntlets
entries: These gauntlets are crafted from the bone and hide of a dragon and infused with latent power. While wearing these gauntlets, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC, which you maintain when you Wild Shape or Polymorph yourself into a different form. Additionally, when you deal fire, cold, acid, poison or lightning damage with a spell while wearing these gauntlets, you ignore enemy resistance to the type dealt.

item.name: Starblood of Siberys
entries: This large star-shaped gem is the crystallised blood of a dragon, infused with the latent magic of the progenitor

entries.name: Siberys
entries: While using this item as a spellcasting focus, you gain a +1 bonus to your spell attack rolls and spell saving throw DCs.

entries.name: Elemental Scales
entries: While using this item as a focus, you can spend 1 sorcery point to cast {@spell absorb elements|XGE} as a reaction, or chromatic orb as an action. Both spells use charisma as your spellcasting ability and count as sorcerer spells for you.

entries.name: Celestial Channel
entries: When you cast a spell that deals fire, cold, poison, acid or lightning damage, you can choose for it to deal radiant damage instead.

item.name: Blisterlathe
entries: This curving {@item sickle|PHB} is formed from the giant saber tooth of an abyssal beast attached to a bone handle. While holding this magic weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it, and gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your druid spells. In addition, you can use the {@item sickle|PHB} as a spellcasting focus for your druid spells.
While wielding this {@item sickle|PHB}, you know the {@spell poison spray} cantrip, which you can cast requiring no components as a bonus action after taking the {@action Attack|XPHB} action.

item.name: Insight Engine
entries: This cubic contraption is formed from intricate interlocking clockwork that buzzes and whirs in constant motion. The sections can be rotated and shifted like an impossible puzzle in the hands of a brilliant mind. At its heart sits a crystal eye that occasionally glimpses at you from between the machinery. You can use the {@skill Insight}
Engine as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells, which grants them a +2 bonus to your spell attack rolls and spell saving throw DCs.

entries.name: Eldritch Insight
entries: You can prepare one additional spell each day, which must be from the Warlock spell list, and of a level that you can cast. Additionally, you can cast either arcane eye or contact other plane once per long rest, but cannot choose which powerful being is contacted for you to ask questions of.

entries.name: Mad Designs
entries: You can choose to add your Charisma modifier to all ability checks made using a tool or kit, as well as to Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks.

item.name: Phoenix Vizard
entries: This mask, formed from the blazing feathers of a Phoenix, is endowed with revitalizing flame. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while wearing this item, a wave of rejuvenating fire bursts from your form at the start of your next turn, healing you and every ally within 30ft of you for a number of hit points equal to your character level. Each enemy within 30ft of you must then succeed on a DC17 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 4d8} fire damage, or half on a success. Once this property occurs, it cannot occur again until 3 days have passed.

item.name: Pyregrass Hay
entries: This bundle of fully grown pyregrass still feels hot to the touch. You can spend 1 minute feeding this hay to a creature that you can use as a mount, causing its speeds to increase by 10ft, and consuming the item. Additionally, the creature can move at this speed for up to 12 hours without risking {@condition exhaustion}. These effects last for 24 hours.

item.name: Roiling Raiment
item.reqAttune: by a druid, sorcerer, or wizard
entries: This shimmering flowing robe is imbued with the arcane energies of the Fluxforge. The Raiment has 5 charges.

While wearing it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell saving throw DC and spellcasting ability modifier: {@spell absorb elements|XGE} (1 charge), {@spell warding wind|XGE} (2 charges) or {@spell protection from energy} (3 charges). The Raiment regains 1 charge whenever you take damage from, or cast a spell that deals, fire, cold or lightning damage (excluding cantrips).

item.name: Surge Conduit
entries: This wand is carved from wood that changes colour rapidly and glows with odd magics. While you are holding this wand, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.

Whenever you cast a levelled spell using this wand as an arcane focus, you must roll a {@dice d20}. If the result is equal to or less than the level of the cast spell, you are affected by a wild magic surge, and must roll on the wild magic table.

item.name: Darkworm Dagger
entries: This razor-sharp stiletto is formed from a carefully filed balhannoth tooth, and retains some of the powers of the dark horror from whence it came. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Additionally, you can cast the spell {@spell hallucinatory terrain} once per day using the {@item dagger|PHB} (DC15). When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature within the area of the spell that has not seen through the illusion, the Sneak Attack die become d8s.

item.name: Sightstring
entries: This pitch-black bone bow is wrapped in shadow mastiff fur. You gain a +1 bonus to attack damage rolls with this magical weapon. Additionally, when you hit a target with this weapon under the effects of your Favored Foe ability or hunter's mark, you can force them to make a DC15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you are considered {@condition invisible} to them, and they are considered {@condition deafened} to sounds you make.
They can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of their turns to end the effect. You can force this saving throw once per short rest.

item.name: Emotion Blade
entries: This large translucent crystal resonates with sentient minds, changing colour to match its wielder's emotions.

As a bonus action, you can telepathically command this weapon to expand into a {@item Longsword|PHB}, {@item Greatsword|PHB} or {@item Lance|PHB} with a blade of psionic energy. Whichever form it is in, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, which deals psychic damage instead of piercing or slashing.

item.name: Thought Nexus
entries: This small pink crystal levitates just in front of your forehead, buoyed aloft by your mind. While attuned to this item, you gain telepathy with a range of 20ft. If you already possess innate telepathy, the range of your telepathy instead increases by 20ft. Additionally, while attuned to the Thought Nexus, you can cast the cantrip {@spell message}.

item.name: Frostfire Fists
entries: These knuckledusters are carved from the bones of the Sublimating creatures of the Pinnacles Sublime; each knuckleduster alternates in synchrony between a coating of ice-cold rime and a burning aura of heat-haze. While attuned to these items, you gain a +1 bonus to the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes, and gain immunity to the effects of extreme heat and cold. Additionally, when you hit with an unarmed strike, you can choose for the attack to either deal an additional {@damage 1d6} fire damage, or deal an additional {@damage 1d4} cold damage and reduce the targets speed by 5ft until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Bison Skullcap
entries: This tusked helmet is formed from the plated head of a mithril bison. If you move 20 feet or more in a straight line towards an enemy and attempt to Shove them, you gain advantage on the skill check and can choose to both knock the creature {@condition prone} and move them up to 15ft away from you.

item.name: Razorbrush Jar
entries: This jar is packed full of metallic leaves picked carefully from the razorbrushes of the Ironlands. You can interact with this object to throw it at a point within 30ft of you that you can see, causing the jar to shatter and spill the razor-sharp leaves within. Every creature in a 15ft radius must succeed on a DC 13 dexterity saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 3d8}) piercing damage, or half on a success. The area in that radius then becomes rough terrain, and a creature moving through it takes 4 points of slashing damage for every affected space they move through for 8 hours. This consumes the item.

item.name: Ur'Taash, The First Crown
entries: This brutalistic crowned helmet, formed from inlaid mithril and byeshk, is one of the most prized artifacts of the Dhakaani. The spirit of the legendary bard Jhazaal Dhakaan is bound within the Crown, advising its wearer on the art of leading an Empire.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: The Ur'Taash is a sentient Lawful Neutral item with an Intelligence of 18, a Wisdom of 20 and a Charisma of 26. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60ft.
The spirit of Jhazaal Dhakaan bound to the Crown communicates telepathically with its wearer and can speak and understand all languages.

entries.name: Personality
entries: The soul of Jhazaal Dhakaan, the Duur'kala (Dirge Singer) that united the Six Kings into the Dhakaani Empire, is bound within the Ur'Taash. The Bard seeks to rebuild the shattered Empire, and guides the wearer to do the same by bestowing the magic of the Ur'Taash upon them. Non-Goblinoids that wear the crown are likely to be denied its magical properties unless they can earn Jhazaal's respect or prove their help for the Dhakaani in the long run.

entries.name: Random Properties
entries: The First Crown has the following randomly determined properties:

items: 2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|DMG|minor beneficial} properties
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|DMG|major beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|DMG|minor detrimental} property; any goblinoid attuned to the Ur'Taash is immune to the detrimental effects of this property.

entries.name: Empire's Dream
entries: You can establish a telepathic mind link with any goblinoid within 300ft, as well as to any humanoid wearing a crown ring regardless of plane or distance. The link allows you to speak to and hear the target telepathically, and grants the benefits of Emperor's Mind to the recipient. The link lasts until the goblinoid leaves the 300ft range, you choose to end it, or the humanoid takes off their crown ring.

entries.name: Emperor's Mind
entries: You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain immunity to psychic damage and the feared and {@condition charmed} conditions.
Other creatures cannot read your thoughts or telepathically communicate with you unless you allow it.

entries.name: Uniter's Spirit
entries: The spirit of Jhazaal Dhakaan allows you to understand any speak any language spoken by a Humanoid or Fey. You can roll and add {@dice 1d12} to all Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks.

entries.name: Warlord's Will
entries: As an action, you can channel the will of Jhazaal Dhakaan to lead any number of creatures within 600ft of you into war with a mighty battle cry. Each creature of your choice within this range can move up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks and make a melee or ranged weapon attack with advantage.
Once you use this property, you cannot use it again until you take a short or long rest.

item.name: Crown Ring
entries: While wearing this ring, the bearer of the Ur'Taash can choose to form a telepathic mind link with you over any distance, even if you are on a different plane. This allows you to communicate regardless of language.

item.name: Screaming Shield
entries: The shields of the Ghaalrac are formed from woven hide and sinew that twists into screaming mouths when struck. As a reaction to a melee attack missing you whilst holding this shield, you can cast {@spell thunderwave} (DC13) requiring no components once per long rest.

item.name: Webduke's Whistle
entries: This small shining whistle is carved from the bark of a Lustrewood tree. As a bonus action, you can blow this shrill whistle to give every creature of your choice within

30ft of you the effects of the spell jump once per long rest. Additionally, you can breathe underwater and gain the ability to speak Bullywug, with which you can communicate simple concepts to frogs and toads.

item.name: Lustrewood Symbol
entries: This small shining whistle is carved from the bark of a Lustrewood tree. While you wear this holy symbol, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your class spells.

Additionally, as a bonus action while wearing this symbol, you can adopt a holy form that visually represents your faith for 1 minute. While the form is active, you have advantage on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks, and can choose to deal radiant or necrotic damage equal to your proficiency bonus to a creature when you hit it with a weapon attack or deal damage to it with a spell.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Every time you use the Symbol to adopt a holy form, roll a {@dice D20}. If your roll is equal to or under 1 + the cumulative number of times you have used this property, your race changes to an Aasimar of a fitting sub-race chosen by your DM. Your ability scores do not change if this occurs. This can be undone only by divine favor, the spell {@spell greater restoration}, or other powerful magic.

item.name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Jade Toad
entries: This carved jade statuette of a frog is small enough to fit in a pocket. If you use an action to speak the command word and throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a Giant Toad for up to 8 hours. If the space where the Toad would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the Toad, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The {@creature giant toad} is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the Toad defends itself but takes no other actions.

At the end of the duration, the Toad reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the Toad becomes a figurine again, this property can't be used again until 3 days have passed.

item.name: Derro Drysuit
entries: This thin bodysuit is made entirely of scaled Stoneswimmer hide enchanted with magic by Derro priests of Orlassk. While wearing this drysuit you gain 30ft {@sense tremorsense|MM}.

Additionally, you can burrow through unworked stone that isn't part of a creature at a speed equal to twice your Strength ability score in feet for 1 minute each day by whispering the name of the Still Lord. While inside stone, your {@sense tremorsense|MM} increases to 60ft.

If the effect ends (or you to choose to end it at will as part of your movement) with you still inside the stone, you are shunted out {@condition prone} into the closest available space, taking {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage for every 5ft that you move through stone. Any creature adjacent to that space must succeed on a DC15 Dexterity saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} and take that damage as well.

item.name: Hook of Earthly Wonders
entries: This Derro fishing hook is formed from small fragments of magnetic metal melted together haphazardly. For it to function, the metal hook must be tied to the end of a fishing line and immersed in enough solid unworked stone to fill at least a 10-foot cube (which it phases through). At the end of each uninterrupted hour of immersion, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, the lure becomes noticeably heavier, having pulled a magical gemstone or metal fragment from somewhere far beyond. The properties of the catch are determined by rolling on the table below. Once the hook catches something, it can't do so again until the next dawn, at which point any previous catch disintegrates into dust.

item.name: The Sublime Spear
entries: This intimidating {@item spear|PHB} has a metal tip with one edge rimed in frost and the other glowing red-hot with heat. When you hit an enemy with this magical {@item spear|PHB}, they take an additional {@damage 1d4} damage, which you can choose to be either fire or cold upon hitting.
You can additionally cast the cantrip control flames using the {@item spear|PHB} as a focus.

item.name: Sanguine Carcanet
entries: This necklace of gold and obsidian has a blood-red ruby at the centre, and smells faintly of blood. Once per day when you are reduced to below half your maximum hit points, you can choose to regain a number of sorcery points equal to your proficiency bonus.

item.name: Shapeless Shield
entries: This shield is carved from the oak trees of the Saavra Forest, and visibly changes colour every day to match a different season. When you use an ability or spell to change into a form that uses a different stat block to your normal one, this shield transforms to armor part of the form you take, granting it the normal +2 bonus to AC.

item.name: Flameblessed Shield
entries: This shield, wielded by a paladin of the Ghaash'kala or Shulassakar, is warded against the presence of fiends. While wielding this shield, you can perfectly recall any path you have travelled, even in magical labyrinths. Additionally, Fiends have disadvantage on all saving throws against your Paladin spells.

item.name: Chainseal Collar
entries: This enchanted collar, made to fit around the neck of a familiar, is crafted from the hide of a skulk and originates from Ohr Kaluun. When worn by a Familiar from the {@spell find familiar} spell, the range of telepathy between you and your Familiar increases to 500 feet. Additionally, once per short rest when your Familiar would take damage, you can use your reaction to take the damage instead. If your familiar is slain, the Collar disappears with it until it is resummoned.

item.name: Carrion Cleaver
entries: This rusted blood-soaked {@item longsword|PHB} was wielded by a barbarian from the Carrion tribes of the Demon Wastes. While wielding this magical {@item longsword|PHB}, you can choose to force every enemy within 15ft of you to make a DC13 Wisdom saving throw when you Rage; on a failure, they are {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Lhurusk Mur
entries: The Duur'kala (Dirge Singers) of the Heirs of Dhakaan channel the spirit of their people to inspire their fellow warriors in battle, bolstering their spirit. Dhakaani lieutenants often hold war horns on their belts that replicate part of this ability, known as Lhurusk Mur. When you provide a {@condition frightened} creature with Bardic Inspiration using this warhorn, they can immediately use their reaction to make a saving throw to end the effect.

item.name: Dashoor Shaarat
entries: The masterwork weapons of the Dhakaani swordsmiths blend rare metals into powerful alloys; the Dashoor Shaarat are blades that blend the strength of adamantine with the anti-aberration effects of byeshk to devastating effect. While wielding this weapon, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. Any attack with this weapon against an object is considered a critical hit. Additionally, Aberrations hit by this weapon take an additional {@damage 1d6} slashing damage and cannot regenerate hit points until the start of your next turn.
When you use your Second Wind whilst wielding this sword, you can immediately move up to half your speed and make a single attack with the Dashoor Shaarat.

item.name: Hektula's Bathrobe
entries: This immaculate bathrobe is formed from the fluffy fur of rakshasa that didn't return their books in time and a shimmering hem of couatl feathers. While wearing this fashionable bathrobe, you gain a +5 bonus to your AC, learn the {@spell prestidigitation} cantrip, and can cast {@spell catnap|XGE} once per long rest. You are additionally immune to the effects of extreme heat and cold. The Bathrobe never gets dirty.

entries.name: Curse
entries: After attuning to this Bathrobe, you are unable to take it off your body in any way short of a {@spell wish} spell or entirely reducing yourself to dust. While you are wearing the Bathrobe, Hektula the Bloody Scribe can cast {@spell scrying} on you at will. You automatically fail the saving throw. The curse can be ended by returning the robe. In person.

Woven from the raw dreamstuff of the Ganti'Uul, this armor subtly changes hue based on your emotions. While wearing this armor, you have advantage on all saving throws made to avoid being {@condition frightened} and immunity to the {@spell dream} spell. Additionally, you gain advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to see through illusions.

This pale chitinous armor is formed from stitchedtogether sections of phase spider carapace. While wearing this armor, you can cast the spell {@spell blink} as an action once per long rest without requiring any components. Additionally, you ignore movement restrictions caused by webbing.

This sickly green and purple armor is crafted from the toxic hides of the beasts of the Abyssal Forest. While wearing this armor, you gain immunity to the {@condition poisoned} condition. Additionally, when a creature damages you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to cause poison to burst from the armor. The attacking creature must succeed on a DC15 Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d8} poison damage, or half on a success. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more is additionally {@condition poisoned} until the start of its next turn.

This set of gilded armor is entirely inscribed with glyphs and arcane runes; As a result, the armor can be given 1 additional infusion by an Artificer compared to normal; The attunement requirement for the additional infusion is ignored if the armor has 2 infusions imbued within it in this way.

This dark glittering armor appears to be formed entirely from sharp, red-tinted obsidian. You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor. You can use your reaction to cast {@spell hellish rebuke} using this armor (DC 15), but are also subjected to the effects of the spell, and must make the saving throw as well.
